response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, ensuring that every resident has the opportunity to participate. She divides the 24 residents into 4 groups, with each group containing 6 participants. This allows for personalized attention and support during the session. 

Emily plans to allocate 3 writing prompts per group for the session. With 4 groups, this means she will need 3 × 4 = 12 initial writing prompts. 

In addition, if the groups finish their initial prompts and want to try more, Emily has prepared an extra set of 10 prompts. These prompts will be shared equally among all the groups. 

Including both the initial and extra prompts, Emily will need a total of 12 + 10 = 22 writing prompts. 

To ensure the prompts are easily accessible, Emily keeps them in a well-organized library, and she also encourages the residents to explore the library of ideas they can draw from during the session. 

The END.
